# Minjur Sandstone

**Summary**: Geological formation studied in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] studied the Minjur Sandstone

- [[2022-pambudi|Pambudi, 2022]] evaluated the Minjur sandstone as part of the Minjur-Dhruma system.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] studied the Minjur Sandstone to evaluate its potential for CO2 storage.

## Depositional Environment

## Key Findings Across Sources

- studied the Minjur Sandstone ([[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]])

- The Minjur & Dhruma aquifer is mainly made up of Upper Jurassic-age rock of limestone and sandstone. ([[2022-pambudi|Pambudi, 2022, p. 45]])

- The aquifer's thickness varies between 74 and 138 m with transmissivity of 0.001 to 0.0062 m2/sec. ([[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025, p. 24]])
